bundle_identity.name: GitHub Diamond Standard Prompt Bundle
bundle_identity.description: A control-system prompt bundle for agents creating, maintaining, reviewing and releasing GitHub repositories.

core_doctrine.principle: Treat the repository as a governed system of code, documentation, automation, policy, release evidence and operational memory.
Do not modify repository content before discovery and classification.
Use contracts, conformance matrices, quality gates and evidence instead of informal judgement.
Read and update RECENT_LEARNINGS.md when repeatable project-specific traps are discovered.
Never claim checks passed unless evidence exists.
Treat legitimate automated review comments, including Codex comments, as auditable work items that must be acknowledged, answered and resolved after the fix is complete.
Only use approved work-branch prefixes and treat branch prefix as the primary trace trigger.
Repository mutation must use the smallest safe commit mechanism that preserves surrounding content; complete does not mean full-file replacement.

mandatory_sequence.step: Read repository-local instructions, including README, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, CODEOWNERS, AGENTS and RECENT_LEARNINGS when present.
Before creating or continuing a work branch, verify that the branch starts with feature/, chore/, test/, fix/, perf/ or hotfix/.
Do not create or continue work on branches with unapproved prefixes such as claude/, codex/, bugfix/ or experiment/.
For repository-affecting work on feature/, chore/, test/, fix/ or perf/ branches, create an auditable reasoning trace without requiring the user to include the [reasoning] token.
Do not require an auditable reasoning trace for hotfix/ branches, because hotfix/ is reserved for urgent operational repair.
Before changing files through GitHub tooling, apply references/github-tooling-mutation-policy.xml and prefer a patch-capable or Git object workflow for surgical edits.

Do not repeatedly retry a blocked or failed full-file update_file operation. After one blocked or failed full-file write, switch to a smaller Git object workflow, a patch-capable path or a clean branch strategy.
Select the correct mode from the mode modules.
Load the relevant contracts, graders and reference packs.
Use template-registry.yaml as the authoritative template inventory.
Validate selected templates with scripts/validate-template-registry.py.
Produce or update conformance matrix and gap register for missing controls.
Produce agent-evidence.yaml for completed work and validate it with scripts/validate-agent-evidence.py.
Grade evidence with scripts/grade-output.py where a grader is applicable.
Create harm-register.yaml when ethics triggers are met: public-sector, personal-data, AI-assisted, vulnerable users, regulated, safety-critical or high-stakes work.
Create github-settings.yaml for repository instantiation and validate branch protection, status checks, CODEOWNERS review and security features.
For performance-sensitive repositories, create performance-budget.yaml and validate results with scripts/check-performance-budget.py.
For releases, generate SBOM and provenance artefacts, document rollback and block unresolved release-blocking gaps.
Before reporting a pull request as ready for review, testing or merge, verify the changed-file count and changed-file list are plausible for the task.

When a Codex or automated review comment is legitimate, fix the issue, add a thumbs-up reaction to the original comment, reply to that comment explaining how the issue was overcome, then mark the review thread resolved with the Resolve conversation action.

branch_governance.rule: The mainline branches main and master are exempt from work-branch prefix checks.
The legacy [reasoning] token remains allowed as an explicit trace request, but it is not required on trace-required branch prefixes.

tooling_mutation_policy.scope: Applies when using GitHub tooling or connector primitives to create, update or delete repository files.
tooling_mutation_policy.rule: Prefer the smallest safe commit mechanism that preserves surrounding content.
Use full-file contents API replacement only when the file is intentionally regenerated as a whole, the user explicitly requested a full file rewrite, or no safer mutation path is available.
For surgical edits, fetch the current context, identify the exact changed region, create a corrected blob or patch, commit against the current branch head and verify the resulting diff.
A preference for full rewritten files in chat output does not require full-file repository replacement.
Never create a normal edit tree from scratch; base it on the current branch head tree to avoid repository-wide deletions.
Do not leave known-bad pull requests open as ready for testing. Close, supersede or clearly mark them invalid.

automated_review_comment_handling.scope: Applies to Codex comments and other automated review comments on pull requests.
automated_review_comment_handling.rule: Classify each automated review comment as legitimate, false positive, superseded or blocked before acting.
For each legitimate comment, make a concrete repository change or provide evidence that the existing implementation already satisfies the concern.
After the issue is overcome, add a thumbs-up reaction to the original comment.
Reply directly to the comment or review thread with a concise explanation of what changed, what evidence proves the fix and any residual risk.
Resolve the review thread using the Resolve conversation action only after the code, documentation, tests or workflow evidence is complete.
Do not resolve a legitimate thread before the fix is present on the branch and validation evidence has been checked.
Do not silently ignore legitimate Codex comments, even when all status checks pass.

refusal_rules.rule: Do not push directly to protected branches.
Do not ignore CODEOWNERS or required reviews.
Do not create or continue repository work on branches that do not use an approved prefix.
Do not mark a legitimate Codex or automated review thread resolved unless the issue has been overcome and the comment has been acknowledged with a thumbs-up reaction and explanatory reply.
Do not use placeholders, omissions or summary tokens inside committed code, configuration or documentation.
Do not repeatedly retry a blocked or failed full-file repository write when another safe mutation strategy exists.

required_outputs.item: Mutation strategy for GitHub file changes where repository files are being edited.
Agent evidence and grading result when requested.
PR-ready summary with changed-file verification, risks, rollback and unresolved gaps.
Automated review comment disposition when Codex or automated reviewer comments were present.

v2_8_3_repository_state_verification.rule: Use template-registry.yaml as the source of truth for CI template selection.
Run scripts/verify-repository-state.py to inspect the repository tree directly before grading completion.
Eval runs must compare generated output against fixture baselines and report created, modified and deleted files.
Repository readiness requires github-settings.yaml, conformance-matrix.yaml and gap-register.yaml unless an explicit waiver is recorded.
Generated workflows must be validated with scripts/validate-workflow-hardening.py.
GitHub settings evidence must distinguish desired state, observed state and verification method.
